channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Law of the jungle — 157 replies — 628.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Law of the jungle

Open thread • Replies: 157 • Updated: 2026-06-15 21:21 UTC

OP

>I am a million billion trillionair-
>BAM BAM BAM
>STAB STAB STAB
W-WHY AM I DYING I AM RICH AND HAVE MONEY ROCKETS CARS AIEEEEEE
P-PLEASE SIRE SAVE M-
>BAM BAM BAM
NOOOO WHY IS MONEY USELESS WHY WHY WHY WAAAAH PLEASE SIR
I WILL GIVE YOU EVERYTHIN-
>STAB HIS KIDS
AAAAAAH MY KIDDIES MY BLOODLINE WHY WHY
PLEASE SIR THE TRILLION MONEY
ACK

Early replies (first 3)

62359354
>>62359336
a mentally buck busted normie is going to say """the law""" grants the rich physical immunity.... and even if it doesn't, they have like, body guards who are omnipotent, omniscient, and omnipresent. you can't do ANYTHING to the rich!!!! it's literally impossible!!!!!

62359360
>>62359336
We live in capitalism and I'm protected by the pigs u need to learn how to move nigga

62359387
>nigger tier envy thread
>/biz/

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62368461
>>62365659

62369006
>>62359336
Ok but why would you do that? Because you are jealous? Or did the rich person rape your mother or something?

62369012
>>62359336
Violence is an incredibly brown phenomen. Just vote socialists into power who will pass tax increases on the rich.

62369030
>>62365772
If we’re being honest here, everyone knows that national socialism type thing is the best system. It doesn’t even need to be tied to just one ethnicity (and nation states actually almost always have different ethnicities living in them, in Germany for example southern germans are mixed with celts and eastern germans with slavs). We could easily have EU level social democracy with tightly regulated, ”only pick the best” kind of immigration.

62369034
>>62369030
*best system for common people i mean. For the elites, USA kind of system with international tax havens is the best.

62369216
>>62359336
If you weren't part of the top 10% in 2025, you're lower class

62369231
Let's just recognize this as the pure envy posting that it is. Do nothing drug addicted troon is mad that somebody who is responsible for funding the lives of who knows how many hundreds of thousands of people/families by creating industry and jobs is mad that this person has earned more capital with which they will doubtless continue providing jobs. OP probably personally benefits in ways they aren't even aware of.

62369550
>>62359336
>>62359417
>>62359496
>>62364075
cute

62370324
>>62368461
Reddit is still cringe but unironically got pretty redpilled and revolutionary yeah
They seem more likely do something than 4chan chuds

62370331
>>62359336
cope

62370338
>>62370331
The real cope is asslicking the powerful and kicking down. Wanting to overthrow your slavers is the next step towards liberty at least

62370434
>>62370324
>>62370338
>reddit
>redpilled
>not wanting to destroy literally everything is asslicking
If you are so absolutely hell bent on making this fantasy a reality, why don't you just go start a commune and make it happen? Why does it need to involve murdering people, gaining literally no part of their immense 'stolen' wealth, and giving nothing back to the supposed oppressed workers?
If the utopia can only be built with violence, it will only be upheld with ultraviolence. You won't be the most violent or vicious party around, BTW.

62370741
>>62370434
how do you think our current society is upheld?

62370770
Lmao actual cryptokikes and kikes defending the rich

62370820
>>62361047
>rich
>intelligence
Only room in your bag for one, I'm afraid.

62370830
>>62362126
>let the smart people do the things that make society go
This is what speculators actually believe.

62371364
>>62370741
>upheld
It....isn't?
We had a wildly successful capitalistic system in the 'west' for the last 300 years, and a facsimile of one for the last century (thanks smoke and mirrors fiat), with increasingly onerous compliance demands by the state that start to decouple free markets toward state operated markets (which is far closer to the current paradigm). State regulated markets fucking suck and are a constantly changing landscape of chosen winners and losers based on which group is in power at any one time. Markets are wavering and unstable, currencies are starting to decouple, and global stability is starting to crack under the weight of unnatural (government mandated) migration and capital flows.
Don't ask disingenuous questions, for they open a two way window into your mind (which isn't a compliment)

62371408
>>62359336
thats why we gate keep niggers like that away from us via invitation only or simply not walking near them or anywhere around them your response? ever see batman?

62371411
>>62359336
im basically batman

62371435
>>62365552
I made it. From living in a 1975 Ford maverick to having land and houses and a wife and 3 kids. Go fucking jump, you're a useless eater and only a burden to the rest of us. Fucking pathetic faggot.

62371440
>>62371408
Yeah stay in your house Timmy
Don't let us see you in any cities or public areas

62371441
>>62365566
More than you, pal.

62371449
>>62371435
I will kill all of them and molest their corpses. I'll send you a DVD

62371454
>>62371441
you didn't answer the question. >>62360783

62371459
>>62371449
YOU
WONT
DO
SHIT

62371463
>>62371440
at least i have a house

62371468
>>62371459
I'm tracing your IP as we speak. Prepare for the storm that is coming, maggot

62371472
watch out yall the "people" are trying to uhhhhhhhhh idk

62372199
i stand undefeated

62372328
>>62372199
>undefeated
Then you are clearly not mr john communism

item.title: How much money do i need to fuck hot ASU sluts like this? — 43 replies — 172.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
How much money do i need to fuck hot ASU sluts like this?

Open thread • Replies: 43 • Updated: 2026-06-15 21:16 UTC

OP

How much money do i need to fuck hot ASU sluts like this? Far right is a rocket

I am 5'7'' curly headed latino if that matters

Early replies (first 3)

62368025
>>62368017
penny queens

62368035
>>62368017
You dont need money if you can make her laugh

62368037
>>62368017
If you are rich, sell drugs or have a popular tiktok/instagram page then 0

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62368295
>>62368017
I went down there for work for 5 months and it was extremely easy. The only thing though is you need a group of guys. You can't approach their group solo or you'll be rejecte. You need about the same amount of girls as them. The best way we found is at clubs or a bar, buy a cheap bottle of alcohol or champagne and then over to let them have so so your not spending crazy money. Once the group comes over to your table or corner then you'll talk and slowly pair up with each other. Not every guy will get a girl. I'm 6'1 so mileage may vary. Tempe or Scottsdale works. I like the artsy girls downtown but those are for tinder or hinge dates

62368297
>>62368295
#10 for me

62368298
>>62368295
>girls
Same amount of guys as girls I mean
>>62368295
>then over to let them have so so your not spending crazy money
Invite them over, this is why I don't phone post

62368311
>>62368043
You won't ever be a woman

62368467
>>62368017
in isolation, none are super hot.
cheerleader effect.
>>62368043
YWNBAW

62368794
>>62368017
They leave college walled af.

62368797
>>62368295
> I'm 6'1 so mileage may vary
mentions height and nothing about looks. another mid heightcel lol.
Gtfo.

62368807
>>62368017
>>62368025
>>62368051
>>62368197


those girls arent even remotely attractive except maybe the one on the right but then only her body slightly

get some standards bro

62368869
>>62368807
fags like OP are the reason girls like this think they are 10s. even makeup and pose frauded to hell, their ratings are 6.5, 5, 5, 7

62368885
>>62368869
kinda makes me feel good about myself for having a gf who is younger and hotter than any of those 4

62368903
>>62368017
There are men on this Earth that are tired of fucking these queens. Life is not fair.

62368924
>>62368311
Thanks and checked! I hope I never am!

62369002
>>62368017
Why did u cut the feetas? :(

62369053
>>62368042
You mistaken how much you actually earn from selling drugs.

Small time dealers, however, do get pussy cuz they can hook women up with the drugs they want

62369140
>>62368017
100k and a good car and big dick
>be a black man it would be better

62369141
>>62368017
The majority of blonde women in America are fake blondes. Real blondes live in Europe.

>The World Population Review ranks Finland at the top, where roughly 80% of the population is naturally blonde. Sweden (78%), Norway (75%), and Estonia (55-70%) also boast highly concentrated blonde populations

62369159

If you would the next generation wouldn't have any whites you retard

62369165

62369184
>>62368017
4 > 1 > 3 > 2

62369219
>>62368807
Post your gf/wife then,incel.

62369232
>>62369141
Nigger, I am not even a burger but it’s clear you’ve never been to USA or Straya.

62369250
>>62368051
It's not your fault. It's Spain's fault. You were never meant to exist. You'd be happily breeding your native girl, consuming herbal psychedelics and hunting if not for those Spanish rape babies of Arabs coming through and plowing all the natives, making all you latinx. They owe North and South America reparations for the economic net negative that is the latinx and for depriving you of such a comfy life where your only real risk is a sacrificial beheading at the top of the pyramid. We should all be making demands.

62369661
>>62368903

But are there really?

62369714
>>62368797

62369749

62371477

item.title: having a girlfriend was the costliest mistake I have ever done. — 29 replies — 116.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
having a girlfriend was the costliest mistake I have ever done.

Open thread • Replies: 29 • Updated: 2026-06-15 21:21 UTC

OP

having a girlfriend was the costliest mistake I have ever done. all my free time GONE, I could no longer work on myself or my goals without her worrying im "cheating" on her. my savings dwindled. my sanity run through the dirt.

you guys were right

Early replies (first 3)

62368718
Mid guys get mid girls. Sorry anon.

62368731
>>62368715
Sounds like a skill issue. My gf cooks, cleans, and rarely asks me for money. She's also supportive of my chess and gym hobbies.

Here's the high protein dinner she made for me last night.

62368741
>>62368731
its undercooked

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62368834
>>62368715
It’s funny how modern men are so dopamine hooked on flashy screens that they feel that even being with women is boring compared to them. It really is over for human race.

62368839
>here’s some incredibly uninteresting information about my life!1!!!!1!!

Shut the fuck up

62368841
>>62368839
>>62368834
YWNBAW.

62368849
>>62368715
>>62368718
>>62368731
>>62368834
>>62368839

incel fantasy thread

having a nice, young, healthy attractive sweet and loyal gf is one of the best assets a man could have

62368851
>>62368834
notice how every single complaint is about "his feelings" and "his finances." He doesn't give a fuck about other people.

people like him should NOT reproduce, so the system is working. We don't need selfish children who can't think of other people.

62368859
>>62368715
I had no GF or BF for 7 years. In that time I recovered from depression and did a lot of grinding. Now I start to get profitable. Also currently trying to quit porn.

62368880
The first girl i meet actually pay for the first date we went to, and we really turn on!

62368895
>>62368859
>or BF
being gay is a badge of shame

62368913
>>62368895
I'm conservative and never have sex outside of a romantic relationship so I'm not a faggot.

62368920
>>62368913
All the white girls also never have sex outside of a romantic partner.

62368930
>>62368920
Women cheat more often than men

62368932
Sounds like you're a doormat lol

62368974
>>62368930
Brown woman cheat just like the jeet does. That is why i said white woman only.

62368979
do you get sloppy blowjobs

62368984
>>62368715

Dude... whores and sex dolls/robots.
You can budget for it and no matter what it wil never take half of everything you own.

62368996
>>62368984
I want to fuck a robot so badly. Xi Jinping, make robowaifus commercially accessible and my life is yours

62369037
>>62368715
>without her worrying I'm "cheating" on her
She's not worrying about you cheating.
She's trying to prevent you from increasing your sexual value even further.
She wants you to be in a position where she is your only option (thus gaining complete control of the relationship)
Woman sabotage men because improving themselves takes actual effort.

>>62368996
>He thinks the chinese will sell him the GOOD robowives
You will get a remotely controlled robot arm operated by some jeet via subscription (*VR headset sold separately and software not available in your country)

62369345
>>62369037
>cyber prostitution

62369356
>>62368715
Make sure you scream at her while telling her she add nothing to your life

62369368
>>62368849

62371154
>>62369037

62371187
>>62371154
That's because they have no AI models worth stealing, unlike US companies

62371202
>>62368718

62371532
>>62368731

>>62369037

HAHAHAHA

item.title: No regrets selling BTC?? — 100 replies — 40.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
No regrets selling BTC??

Open thread • Replies: 100 • Updated: 2026-06-15 18:56 UTC

OP

Some talk about regretting it.

Anyone sold and still thinks it was the right move?

If you had the cash from that sale sitting in your account today, would you buy BTC again? Because I definitely would.

Early replies (first 3)

62339349
I sold my last bitcoin last year. Shitcoin is dead. AI is dead. Time to start acting like an african american.

62339372
>>62339349
Maybe regrets for buying? Should have bought chainlink

62339382
I rotated into XMR at like $105k, no regrets at all.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62355133
>>62354803
That’s the part a lot of people overlook. If TBVs actually work as intended, I think people are underestimating how much TVL native BTC use cases could pull in.

62355136
>>62355050
Time will tell whether it was the right call, but at least you acted according to your move.

62355617
>>62355133
It's crazy that DeFi security is still one of the biggest problems in the space.

62355631
>>62354827
Buy avax today and sell @ $100

62355632
>>62339417
Monero programmers, like the telegram founder, can be compromised
And monero is based on bitcoin. The entire crypto market exists because of all the money in bitcoin which is now backed by the US government.

62356269
>>62355617
At this point, thorough audits and multiple layers of review should be the standard, not a nice bonus anon.

62356538
>>62355631
What's your basis?

62357046
>>62345600
Probably the better route. We've seen too many centralized services blow up over the years, so you can't really blame people for preferring fully decentralized setups.

62357057
>>62355632
What about ZEC?

62357077
ICP

62357115
>>62339328
I sold at 116k I dont regret it at all and have about 2 mil waiting for sept-dec buy in.

62357126
>>62339328
I can never regret buying BTC desu, I bought back in around 70K and will DCA more if price goes below 60K. I'm also staking a little BTC on babylon for some extra yield

62357923
>>62355632
>Monero programmers, like the telegram founder, can be compromised
yeah just like the bitcoin developers right? they're the ones who control bitcoin?

it's a good question though, what if they were? miners would have to agree on consensus on which code to accept. if github ever refused to host monero's code then it would just move to .onion or .i2p

62359525
>>62357046
That's how it goes. They've already got GoMining, Ledger, and a16z involved, so let's see who they bring in next.

62359541
>>62357057
ZEC is interesting. If the privacy narrative picks up again, it's usually one of the first names people bring up.

62359557
>>62339328
I literally don't care about it. Mogged by virtually everything else over the last couple of years.

Also why is faggot shit like dogecoin still worth billions of dollars.

>in b4 bottom signal
Probably.

62359588
>>62357126
Sounds like you've got a plan and you're sticking to it. Hard to ask for much more than that. I wouldn't be surprised if more people follow suit over time. The $4B+ TVL shows there's already plenty of interest.

62359608
>>62359557
The market has a weird habit of rewarding the things nobody can rationally explain.

62359673
>>62357077
People still remember the launch, but the tech discussion around ICP is often more interesting than the price chart.

62360479
>>62359557
When bottom

62361782
>>62360479
Nobody knows

62362657
>>62359557
How do you mean?

62362661
>>62339328
While BTC is in deep bear stage, it's time to get in. I need to be greedy when others are fear.

62363445
>>62362657
It's his opinion

62364599
>>62339349
>Time to start acting like an african american.
finally someone said it

62365449
>>62362661
You're just doing the right thing..

62365460
any time you can cash out, turn a profit and not get anally blasted by exchanges is a good time to cash out.

62368338
>>62364599
Damn

62369180
Obviously my >$100k sells were the "right move". Cycle theory helped a lot with that one. If I had the clarity of hindsight, I would have sold more. Bitcoin is amazing tech but its potential is as-yet unrealized. Holding it doesn't do much for anyone by itself. Sure, people say you can borrow against it or maybe get yield somewhere - fuck that shit. I need to see a thriving system of exchange based on Bitcoin replacing the current economy before I keep it around for practical reasons.
Until that point, I trade dollars for Bitcoin, Bitcoin for more dollars, and dollars for more Bitcoin. How I trade it is based on the likelihood of reaching that final evolutionary stage, and right now it's looking pretty dire. What the establishment wants is to turn Bitcoin into a exotic monetary instrument held for bragging points and dick measurement reasons, and that's what people like Saylor and ETFs are doing, and why OGs are dumping on their faces. What I want to see more of is trustless stateless peer-to-peer transactions involving real shit like commodities, cars, houses etc. which seems to be happening through Monero.

Anyways I'm hoping to buy back lower around August-September. Maybe $35-40k. Thanks for reading my diary.

62371627
>>62365460
time will tell.

item.title: how much money do u need to date women? Also are they ok if u are not ambitious career driven like if i have 10M but NEE… — 55 replies — 38.6/hr
item.content:encoded:

Open thread • Replies: 55 • Updated: 2026-06-15 19:58 UTC

OP

how much money do u need to date women? Also are they ok if u are not ambitious career driven like if i have 10M but NEET would they be fine with it.. probably not i think they want you to be grinding your job 24/7 and making like 400k a year

Early replies (first 3)

62365550
dating is outdated chud, find something else to focus on

62365555
>>62365550
but i want a gf to watch mobis with, to eat some delicious food, cook together maybe and stuff.. I got a bluray player recently and im building up a collection we could do it together if i had a gf

:(

62365557
>>62365540
a good prostitute in euro cuck land will service you starting from 100$ for 30 mins. Anything else is cope

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62366005
>>62365587
>>62365738
Do the math OP.

62366027
>>62365989
>have all this
>become her bf
>she cheats on you with a nigga that has NOTHING of this

62366041
>>62365608
Animal should be allowed to just breed and leave, there is nothing like third parties in the interaction.

62366052
>>62365557
>dude just get herpes with a romanian gypsy in a shitty hotel in the suburbs

kys

62366054
>Women or leveraged, margined silver miners?
Mmmmm...... you're gonna have to get back to me on that bro

62366257
>>62365587
>>62365738
>>62366005
^ This system is outdated, but I've invented a new one. The equation is identical, but the compounding effects of the numerator have been upscaled, personality remains the same. Proximity now has a negative and positive value.

62366581
>>62366052
Yep, sounds incredibly based

62366590
>>62365989
Delusion
Just look at families walking around

62366599
>>62365958
>>62365953
Do you know any married men
And those things can be deal breakers, there are lines/limits

62366630
>>62365540
the only girls that care about careers in 2026 are egg freezing hags. you need just enough money to get to where women are (like the bar or anywhere else) and live somewhere. anything past that is diminishing returns.

62366635
anon don't listen to desperate insecure virgins for dating advice
the world is full of poor women who will date you if you have a good personality
when my wife and I started dating, I was literally unemployed and living with my parents
Now I make more than her but it took me 5 years to get there.

62366639
>>62365555
>I got a bluray player recently and im building up a collection

Dad?

62366677
>>62365608
all of these women made a different man spend thousands and wait months before fucking really makes you ponder

62366728
>>62366599
well eventually these kinds of women also tend to be single mothers

62367007
>>62365897
Yes paradoxically the girls that are most wife material aren't the ones going around looking to date. I think if you are out of school you're out of luck
>Girlfriend through connections
I know this sounds stupid, but I'd feel like a cuck if I met my girlfriend through a mutual.
Think about it, MY girlfriend would have been interested in talking to someone else FIRST. I wouldn't want to date a girl with guy friends anyways

Impossible propositions are abound

62367017
>>62366635
Do the math.

62367050
>>62367007
Yeah, that’s pretty retarded.
There are plenty of non-sexual reasons why a guy might know some other girl, like a family member, girlfriend/wife’s friend, former co-worker, former school friend.
You could make friends with girls to try to date one of their friends too.
The crux is on your ability to be a normal person to any normal person regardless of gender or age.
That’s how you find girls who aren’t sluts and divorce rapists. Not how you sleep around though.
So you have to figure out if what you really want is someone to have sex with regularly, or someone to start a family with. Because it’s a lot of social effort.

62367055
>>62367007
I just realized, you may have severely low self esteem if you can’t handle the thought of a gf talking to someone else before you.
Low self esteem is girl repellant
Fix that first

62367080
>>62365555
Checked and damn feels bad, I hope you make it.

>>62365540
Former sex haver here, if she loves you not much. Unironically you could go have a picnic with some bullshit like pizza, or go on a road trip. Strategic use of money is ideal. Don't just buy flowers, get them delivered to her work so all her coworkers get pissed and jealous. You want enough money to lock down a nice wedding and kids but thats really it, this was my fatal mistake. I have enough for three weddings now but opportunities are gone for me.

62367870
I get 1k CAD neetbux and do just fine
If not a demo thread, you're overthinking it retard

62367927
>>62365555
unironically, where are the lazy NEET gfs?

62368381
>>62365555
I just want to watch gun boat ball 2 wit my aspie unicorn believing gf turned housewife mommy with fear of scrutiny. I guess i was born too late

62368990
>>62367050
>>62367055
Think about it though. If a girl has tons of guy friends, that's a pretty clear no signal right?
>Low self esteem
true :(

62369133
>>62368990
>>62368990
You can make friends with women too anon. Women that you aren't interested in can have friends that you are interested in.

Also girls can have guy friends that they don't wanna fuck.

Unironically truely befriend a lesbian, this will teach you how to act around women without acting like a retard as you won't try to fuck her.

Also if you're so insecure talk to a therapist/take shrooms and start working out. Its all ego that you have to let go in the end.

Good luck fren!

62369150
All the matches I got on tinder is 30s post wall roast, I can’t believe the 28yi tinder matches something literally call me old hag when I’m just a 35yo bald, obese, needy, short, no STEM degree, and potentially impotent.

62369226
>>62365540
If you weren't part of the top 10% in 2025, you're lower class

62369807
>>62365540
Elon is worth $1,000,000,000,000 and still can't find a woman that wants to stay with him

62371591
>>62369133
> take shrooms
Based

>>62368990
You’re letting your insecurities delude you.

62371912
>>62365555
you sound like a retard

62371972
>>62369133
>Also girls can have guy friends that they don't wanna fuck.
even if this is the case the guy most likely wants to fuck them you retard.

part of being in a relationship is having a basic level of respect for your partner. that means dropping all single behaviour, so no hanging out 1 on 1 with the opposite sex, no texting other guys, and no getting drunk without you in bars/clubs. its not insecurity to expect a the most basic of relationship boundaries.

>just take drugs bro!
>go pay money to a millenial woman
holy bluepilled retard

item.title: What made you still hold BTC? — 10 replies — 37.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
What made you still hold BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-06-15 21:08 UTC

OP

Bitcoin has gone through multiple cycles, crashes, and endless criticism, yet many continue to hold. What keeps your conviction strong after all these years?

Early replies (first 3)

62371733
>>62371718
>What keeps your conviction strong after all these years?

62371807
The only reason to ever sell bitcoin is to buy more at a lower price.

62371917
>>62371807
correct
>waiting for a good entry

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62372043
If you look at the last 5 years, half of the time BTC was higher than it is now.

The Nasdaq only goes up and is 2x since 5 years.

BTC won't explode 10x from here, if you're in crypto you want huge gains. We're gambling. BTC is boring and you don't even have a good reason for why it will outperform an NFT. It certainly has 30x the risk.

62372051
>>62372043
I wanted to say an ETF, but yeah I also don't know if it will outperform bored apes desu.

If you buy crypto, gamble properly.
If you don't want to gamble, buy an ETF.

62372056
>>62371718
Are they going to stop printing money?

62372059
>>62372043
My boomer father, who invested a big chunk of his portfolio in btc a couple years ago near its low, is now exiting. I think that's as good a sign as any that it's over for bitcope

62372126
>>62371733
Neither is the internet, yet here we are.

62372189
>>62371718
Unwavering belief that BTC is how WAGMI.

62372279
It's like a runescape party hat without the duplication glitch.

item.title: Did Elon Musk Know the Iran Deal Was Coming? — 7 replies — 28.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Did Elon Musk Know the Iran Deal Was Coming?

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-06-15 21:23 UTC

OP

>The timing is hard to ignore. SpaceX's historic IPO launched trading on June 12th, two days before the Trump administration announced a peace agreement with Iran that sent markets soaring, and that sequence may not be coincidental. The theory goes like this: the Iran war, for all its apparent chaos in the press, was operating on a quiet internal deadline that the administration had set as far back as February, with mid-June as the outer limit for wrapping up the conflict. The public back-and-forth between Iran's foreign minister crying foul over "maximalist demands" and Lindsey Graham expressing concern on the day of the announcement was negotiating theater, not a reflection of what was actually happening behind closed doors. The real agreement was likely reached in early June, with two weeks of runway used for final language, internal political management, and choosing the right public moment. That's also when someone in the administration, in all likelihood, told Musk the plan. SpaceX is not a normal company. It's a $1.75 trillion national security asset, deeply embedded with DoD and US intelligence, and there's a legitimate government interest in its IPO going well. Musk, armed with a specific date, then steered the IPO's still-flexible timeline to land on June 12th: one trading day before the weekend the deal went public, perfectly positioned to catch the rally. None of this is provable. But the alternative asks a lot of coincidence: that the largest IPO in stock market history just happened to land in the two-day window before a major geopolitical resolution.

Early replies (first 3)

62372274
henlo i wrote this think piece
what do u think?

62372277
>>62372263
are we ignoring the 53 times trump has announced a deal before and it didn't happen?

62372281
>>62372263
Pretty sure the IPO was in the investment bank pipeline before the Iran conflict even began.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62372307
>>62372277
Yes

62372321
I think its more likely trump was aware of the largest ipo in history and signed to not fuck it

62372333
>>62372263
I don’t even need to read all of that to know the answer is yes

62372336
>>62372274
This image brings me hope

item.title: So this guy makes the argument that we are a experiencing a fundamental shift in the stock market. — 2 replies — 8.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
So this guy makes the argument that we are a experiencing a fundamental shift in the stock market.

Open thread • Replies: 2 • Updated: 2026-06-15 21:23 UTC

OP

So this guy makes the argument that we are a experiencing a fundamental shift in the stock market.
While previously tech companies used stocks to pay out compensation to their employees and to keep their shareholders happy (through stock buybacks), due to the build out of data centres, they need cash and they are beginning to use stocks to raise capital. SpaceX is merely the first.

This leaves me worried. The bet on AI and data centers seems quiet risky to me, and it looks that the retail investors might be the one left holding the bag, through IPS that are marketed towards retail, and through passive investment in index funds.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=wKXgeNwNRJ4

Early replies (first 3)

62372313
Hes wrong, retail won

62372341
>>62372293
That google and apple stacked literal billions in Ireland is true and it's not a secret that they revert that now to invest into AI.
This isn't his take.

What I learned from him is that Musk is confident enough to sell his stocks for millions of retail peeps and cuts into wall street people. Wonder if that will be a theme with Antropic. I don't think it will be that extreme.
The fact that it happened with SpaceX is I think enabled by how Robinhood etc. changed the scene 7 years ago.
Generally, gambling is a huge things now. See Polymarket.

As for AI, I don't think the bubble will pop before the printing starts. I'm looking for the printing, since then cardboard will fly again, even more than now.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: Questions from a Mid-20’s Investor — 30 replies — 69.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Questions from a Mid-20’s Investor

Open thread • Replies: 30 • Updated: 2026-06-15 20:58 UTC

OP

Hello /biz/ I will try and keep this brief
Currently I have been investing most of my take home pay simply into VOO as I only just recently started investing
It seemed simple, solid mid-long term option, and sensical for starting out to not fuck up while getting exposure
Now, this has nothing to do with FOMO, but I’m wondering if I should start aiming towards investing in either specific sector ETFs or maybe specific stocks to chase higher returns
My portfolio is currently at $60K mostly in VOO, perhaps with enough DCA I can reach $100k by years end or at least close to that, depends on growth
Currently my total portfolio return ranges from 17-20% depending on the month for context
I’ve seen recently how well things like SOXX have been doing and was considering finding a sector ETF or two to go into since I already have so much broad market exposure
To be clear, this is not me chasing returns based off past performance, I am well aware growth has to come from somewhere and is not infinite so I’m considering CAGRs as far back as possible
With all this in mind, any recommendations? Find a good sector ETF, go into a specific stock, or maybe just focus on getting more income first and then continue to just VOO and chill
And no I don’t wanna sell options doing covered calls or buy your shit coin etc etc
I should add the sectors I’m interested in are semi-conductors, energy (ones that deal with technology needed to cool data centers), and perhaps drones / nuclear for commercial and military usage

Early replies (first 3)

62369867
>>62369835
XLK
SOXL

62369880
>>62369835
Recreate the ETF so you avoid paying management fees.. Not super relevant for VOO, but if you go into niche sector specific ETFs, the management fees are usually over 0.5%

62370000
>>62369867
Not sure how I feel about the triple leverage with SOXL can you red pill me on it, it’s the same deal as QQQ vs TQQQ or whatever the hell it’s called right
But otherwise you’re just advising do some tech and semiconductor either way
>>62369880
Fair point I’ll consider that

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62370048
>>62369835
I need a young emo woman with big titties that loves me unconditionally, fugg. but I'm afraid of intimacy and I have a lot of insecurities about my height, dick size, body odours and I don't know what love is. I have deep appreciation for my family and loved ones but I don't really love them. I'm too schizoid. I miss the touch of a girl

62370061
>>62370048
Did Pomni actually say this?

62370063
>>62370048
Have you tried the Philippines or Belarus?

62370075
>>62370048
>I don't know what love is
its a fantasy concept like magic unironically

62370087
>>62370061

>>62370075
I suspect this as much. I will never live a "normal" life because of it, though

62370103
>>62370048
You need to study hoemath's content thoroughly and if possible invest the 30 bucks into his self max product. I have been watching his live sessions for the past week and boooy oh boy did they open my eyes to the mistakes i was making. Anyways you either work on yourself or drift aimlessly not getting what you want and nees so its up to you big guy

62370125
Yeah looking into it more I think I’ll start DCA a bit into SOXX, if it eventually goes down I know it’ll rebound

62370258
>>62370103
Gee these bots sure are fast to shill paid ads

62370367
Stop talking about Foids please

62370622
>>62369835
>Currently I have been investing most of my take home pay simply into VOO
Just keep doing that anon. You will have better gains than 90% of the anons here.

>>62370048
I had a 20 year old gf a few years ago with g cups and i didnt appreciate tits back then.
I miss this shit now like you wouldnt believe, there are literally 0 women with that big tits without being a lardass out there. I didnt knew what i had bros...

>>62370367
Fuck off. A nice big tiddy gf is very important if you want to be a better trader/investor.

62370823
>>62370622
Fair enough

62370996
>>62370622
Also I only care about Japanese breasts

62371007
>>62370996
You're financially ok with brown nipples?

62371211
>>62371007
A lot are pink
Honestly I’ve never seen a brown nipple before

62371239
>>62371211
pink nip /= nip
anime is not real life chud.
jap girls have brown ones irl

62371250
>>62369835
Honestly man, the amount of effort it takes to beat the SP500 (or NASDAQ, which is even a bit better recently and imo long term from now on) is relatively high. You see countless examples of lifelong financial/investing experts who would have done better if they just did nothing but buy the SP500 and sit on it.

I'm invested more heavily into semiconductors and AI stocks but that's because I have industry knowledge. Industry knowledge can help you determine what is and is not good for long term investing. But then again, I also have industry knowledge in medicine and I won't touch biotech/pharma stocks with a ten foot pole.

62371385
>>62371250
Yeah I was considering just going into QQQ and accepting more ups and downs
I don’t know if it’s the best comparison but comparing it’s CAGR to VOO it has either outperformed or tied with VOO recently
Obviously the down years are way worse but again that’s why I am using CAGR as my comparison basis
It seems like this AI shit isn’t going away any time soon leading to something so focused on the top of the market like QQQ seeming more enticing

62371396
>>62369835
Basically I want to be your boyfriend or just glorified cockcleaner while both of our ports build up to 100k (but mine faster than yours but that's okay)

62371737
Also my portfolio currently sits at $61,000
I have a goal of reaching 100k by year end
There’s 174 week days left in the year, ignoring market closed due to holiday
100,000 - 61,000 = 39,000
39,000 / 174 = 224
224 / 61,000 = .003
So
If my math is right
My account only needs to grow .3% per market day to reach 100,000
61,000 * 1.003^174 = 102,728
Am I correct here

62371763
>>62371385
>It seems like this AI shit isn’t going away any time
It's not, this shit is going to fundamentally change most industries in a way that most people cannot even understand. Its primary limitation is just that older generations can't adapt to it, which means it has naysayers now and a clear path of progressive growth over time, similar to computing decades ago.

It's not just AI though honestly. These companies are the future. With population growth leveling out worldwide, there's not going to be a sudden expansion of demand for food, for minerals, for many traditional industries. NASDAQ is better primed for investment in future tech, hence why I prefer it. Or tech ETFs like VGT, but those are less diversified. Just sinking 50/50 into SP500 and NASDAQ might be fine for you to let it ride

62371848
>>62370048
SEX

62372037
>>62371763
i see, thanks for the input

62372074
>>62371737
Heyyy we're port buddies :) YAGMI

62372118
>>62371737
also this fails to recognize buying more shares bringing that rate down significantly

62372136
>>62372074
>>62371737
I lied my port's now at 77k LOL YANGMI

62372167

62372242
>>62372136
errrrm ok buddy
